user_brief: Create an original square logo for the open-source GitHub project 'ansys-aedt-mcp'. The project is a non-commercial Model Context Protocol server for Ansys Electronics Desktop automation through PyAEDT. Visual concept: electromagnetic field lines, circuit/mesh geometry, simulation nodes, and an MCP connector motif. Style: modern technical open-source repository mark, clean vector-like raster logo, high contrast, works on GitHub README light and dark themes. Avoid copying Ansys branding, avoid official trademarks, avoid dense text. Include at most a small readable 'AEDT MCP' mark if text is reliable.

detected_entities.why_it_matters: Repository/project name; the logo should read as a GitHub open-source utility rather than a commercial brand.

detected_entities.name: Ansys Electronics Desktop (AEDT)
detected_entities.why_it_matters: Core platform being automated; visual cues can reference electronics/simulation without copying Ansys branding.

detected_entities.name: PyAEDT
detected_entities.why_it_matters: Python automation layer; suggests scripting/automation motifs and technical precision.

detected_entities.name: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
detected_entities.why_it_matters: Connector/interoperability motif should visually communicate an MCP server and tool integration.

detected_entities.name: GitHub
detected_entities.why_it_matters: Logo must work as a repository avatar/readme mark on light and dark GitHub themes.

detected_entities.name: Ansys
detected_entities.why_it_matters: Must avoid trademark/logo copying while still conveying the electronics simulation domain.

search_queries: Ansys Electronics Desktop AEDT official overview PyAEDT
PyAEDT official documentation Ansys Developer Portal
Model Context Protocol official specification overview
GitHub Ansys open source organization repository AEDT Python Client Package

knowledge_notes.point: AEDT is Ansys Electronics Desktop, a platform for electronics system design and access to EM simulation tools such as HFSS, Maxwell, Q3D Extractor, SIwave, and Icepak.
Ansys Developer documentation states AEDT supports ACT Python scripting and the PyAEDT and PyEDB libraries.
PyAEDT is the Python-facing automation layer for AEDT workflows, enabling simulation orchestration and data exchange.
MCP stands for Model Context Protocol; the spec is versioned and centered on a standardized protocol for tool/context interoperability.
Ansys maintains an open-source GitHub organization, so the project should visually fit an OSS repository identity rather than product packaging.

visual_resource_notes.asset_or_signal: Electromagnetic simulation imagery and product-domain cues (EM, SI/PI, thermal, multiphysics).
visual_resource_notes.visual_use: Use abstract field lines, wave contours, node graphs, or mesh geometry to evoke simulation without depicting any proprietary UI or logo.

visual_resource_notes.asset_or_signal: AEDT as a Python-automated environment for electronics simulation.
visual_resource_notes.visual_use: Use a connector/pipe or linked-node motif to represent automation and server integration.

visual_resource_notes.asset_or_signal: MCP as a standard protocol layer.
visual_resource_notes.visual_use: Use a clean central connector, plug/socket, or interlocking channel motif to imply protocol mediation and tool bridge.
Prioritize a bold, legible icon silhouette with simple geometry and no small details so it remains clear at favicon/avatar scale.

original_reference_notes.reference: Ansys Electronics Desktop / Ansys Electronics official product pages
original_reference_notes.canonical_detail: Official visual identity is corporate and branded; AEDT is a broad simulation suite with electronics/em simulation emphasis.
original_reference_notes.how_to_use_in_prompt: Borrow the domain language and abstraction level only: electromagnetic lines, circuit traces, mesh grids, and simulation nodes; do not reuse Ansys wordmark, color blocks, or product screenshots.

original_reference_notes.canonical_detail: PyAEDT is positioned as Python automation for AEDT workflows.
original_reference_notes.how_to_use_in_prompt: Include subtle Python/automation energy via connected nodes, pipeline arrows, or protocol bridge elements; keep the mark minimal and technical.

original_reference_notes.canonical_detail: Model Context Protocol is an interoperability standard; visual identity should suggest a universal connector or interface layer.
original_reference_notes.how_to_use_in_prompt: Make the center of the logo feel like a protocol bridge—e.g., an elegant node hub, connector glyph, or interlocking ports—rather than a literal product badge.

ambiguities_to_resolve_in_prompt: Whether the logo should lean more toward electronics simulation (field lines/mesh) or toward protocol/connector identity (MCP bridge).
Whether any text should be included at all; if so, keep it extremely short and highly legible at small sizes.
Preferred background treatment: transparent, dark-field, or dual-mode adaptable icon with strong outline.
How abstract the design should be to avoid trademark adjacency while still signaling Ansys/AEDT relevance.

usage_context.reference_policy: use original designs and content as visual reference when they clarify the user's brief

generation_quality_constraints: Square composition optimized for avatar scale and README badges.
Strong silhouette readability at small sizes.
Clean vector-like edges despite raster output.
High contrast for both light and dark themes.
Avoid dense micro-detail, tiny text, and complex scene depth.
Avoid Ansys branding, logos, UI screenshots, and official color/logo mimicry.
Keep the mark original, minimal, and technically credible.

recommended_visual_direction.format: 1:1 square logo, centered icon with optional tiny wordmark
recommended_visual_direction.composition: Single bold emblem: central MCP connector hub with radiating electromagnetic field lines, layered circuit/mesh geometry, and 3-5 simulation nodes around the perimeter; keep a compact, symmetrical silhouette.
recommended_visual_direction.style: Modern technical OSS mark, geometric, crisp vector-like raster, subtle glow or gradient only if needed for separation; no 3D rendering, no photorealism.
recommended_visual_direction.palette: Monochrome or near-monochrome base with one electric accent (cyan/teal or blue-violet); ensure it works on both light and dark backgrounds.
must_avoid: Official trademarks or product UI replicas
Literal cable/USB iconography that feels generic rather than protocol-specific
